title: Sampling, Inference, Regression, and Causal Limits · Mastery Check
summary: Evaluate a program change from sampled observational and experimental evidence. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an inference and limitations brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.
Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: The observed values are 14, 18, 22, 18 minutes. Calculate their mean. Use the result to decide Regression Diagnostics for an inference and limitations brief.
